understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by moving a pushable object (block) onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+', reaching the WIN condition before running out of the per-level step budget, and then continuing until all levels were completed.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions shifted the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction when not blocked by walls or immovable objects; if a pushable block was directly adjacent in the move direction and the space beyond it was free, the block would be pushed along with the selected piece. Walls ('#') acted as impassable boundaries that could fully prevent movement, while the movement_blocked hints only indicated adjacency and were not definitive. When multiple selectables existed, control could be switched by using CLICK at the exact pixel-coordinate position of another selectable; clicking an empty space or a non-selectable (or not matching the precise coordinate) would not change the selected piece.
